evidence.text: Generous time off, a lot of fringe benefits like shoes, clothes, and company outings. Good pay. Good company to work for with great benefits
GE offers the full spectrum of benefits including healthcare, dental, yearly bonuses, etc. Permissive time off allows you to take as much PTO as your manager is okay with. Mediocre benefits
Permissive Time Off Standard Bonus Structure Average Retirement Benefits, Evolving Company with Focus on Green Energy

Excellent team, benefits and just all around company. Opportunity to lean. State of the art technology all the time. Never board!! Team players help each other. Excellent support network. Excellent customers. GE is the BEST!

I got the opportunity to learn a plethora of technical skills in 2 years working with GE that I still use at other employers to this day. The work environment was friendly and encouraging. Never I had a day where I dreaded going into work or encountering an individual. Everyone was held at a high responsibility for their own work and attitude which was a huge plus for those that actually came in to work. Great Learn...

evidence.role: Current employee - Senior Finance Manager - Financial Planning Analysis
evidence.text: Terrible insurance, cheaper to pay cash then use their top tier plan. No work life balance, plan on working weekends and holidays. Unlimited PTO is a nice in theory, but hardly every granted due to high work load and short staffed. No work life balance
They have not so great work life balance Great place to work
Very slow in pay increase and compensation backage is very week. No work life balance

evidence.role: Current employee - Instrumentation and Controls Engineer
evidence.text: Limited Benefit options (I.e. can only choose between three options for health care, can't choose providers in any plan) Good place to work

You do not receive many vacation days or personal days. For higher salary positions, they almost have someone else in mind, so even if you are overqualified with a degree, You will be overlooked and not get the job because they already had someone picked out from the get go. Very toxic work environment. From the top of the management to the people working out on the floor. Favoritism, HR, Union, Procedures all for s...

evidence.role: Current employee - Premium End User Support
evidence.text: Constant question whether your job or even business will continue to exist, not enough workers to handle the workload evenly, constant low-level feeling of anxiety about the future in general. Good place to work if you can handle anxiety

The move to the Grove was unnecessary and expensive. Employees had to pay for parking and the office was eventually closed anyway due to GE Digital's failure. All remaining offices in Miami are closing as of 2023 in favor of other sites such as Monterrey, Mexico. Where there were originally over 100 Miami technicians, there are only about 25. As the office was expanding, a lot of office politics set in. You didn't k...
Leadership blinded by politics, nepotism, historical best practices Global impact

Horrible hours - 80+ hour work weeks Terrible managers - who are clueless about what you do and are only looking for their next role Diversity and inclusion was all smoke and mirrors. Most toxic workplace
strict about attendance and internet connection, a lot of turn over very friendly place to work

The pay is not on the same level as other similar companies in the area. They go by sonority of being with the company more than on talent or capability. The job requirements are not worth doing for what you get paid for. Good Community to Grow, Bad Pay
